concepts.title: The Yalta Conference
concepts.description: The Yalta Conference was a meeting between the leaders of the three main Allied powers (US, UK, and USSR) in February 1945, where they discussed post-war reorganization and the division of Europe.

concepts.title: The Truman Doctrine
concepts.description: The Truman Doctrine was a policy introduced by US President Harry Truman in 1947 to provide economic and military aid to countries threatened by communism, marking the beginning of a new era of US involvement in global politics.

concepts.title: The Iron Curtain
concepts.description: The Iron Curtain was a term used to describe the physical and ideological barrier that separated Western Europe from the Soviet bloc, symbolizing the division of Europe during the Cold War era.

concepts.title: MAD (Mutual Assured Destruction)
concepts.description: MAD is a nuclear strategy where two or more countries possess nuclear weapons that are capable of causing massive destruction, making a direct attack suicidal, thus maintaining a balance of power to prevent conflict.

concepts.title: The Berlin Blockade and Airlift
concepts.description: Students often confuse the Berlin Blockade and Airlift with the Berlin Wall. The Berlin Blockade was a Soviet attempt to cut off Western access to Berlin in 1948-1949, which was countered by the US-led Berlin Airlift.

concepts.title: The Non-Aligned Movement
concepts.description: The Non-Aligned Movement was a group of countries that remained independent and did not align with either the Eastern or Western bloc during the Cold War era, led by countries like India, Yugoslavia, and Egypt.

concepts.title: The Cuban Missile Crisis
concepts.description: The Cuban Missile Crisis was a 13-day standoff between the US and USSR in October 1962, where the US discovered Soviet missile sites in Cuba, leading to a potential nuclear conflict that was eventually resolved through diplomatic negotiations.

concepts.title: The Warsaw Pact
concepts.description: The Warsaw Pact was a military alliance formed in 1955 by the Soviet Union and its Eastern European satellite states to counter the growing influence of NATO, marking a significant escalation of the Cold War.
